_comment: Chronos Earth — war 'campaign' front lines drawn on the globe as you scrub the timeline. Each campaign has dated keyframes; each keyframe has 'front' (the front line, drawn bright white/yellow) and 'area' (the advancing side's controlled territory, shaded). Coordinates are [lon, lat]. 'year' may be fractional (e.g. 1941.95 ≈ Dec 1941). The app snaps to the nearest keyframe as time moves, so playing through the war years animates the front. Add a campaign by copying one and editing the dates and coordinates.

keyframes.label: Oct 1914 — trench line stabilises on the Aisne
Dec 1941 — at the gates of Moscow
Aug 1943 — after Kursk, the tide turns

campaigns.name: Second World War — Liberation of the West

keyframes.label: Aug 1944 — breakout and the liberation of Paris
Dec 1944 — the Battle of the Bulge
334 BCE — crossing into Asia: the Granicus
332 BCE — Tyre falls; Egypt welcomes him
331 BCE — Gaugamela: the Persian Empire falls
326 BCE — the Hydaspes: victory in India
1279 — all China conquered: the largest land empire in history
201 BCE — Carthage beaten: the western Mediterranean
30 BCE — Egypt taken: the Mediterranean is a Roman lake
395 CE — the empire splits, West and East
476 CE — the West falls; the East endures
Feb 1941 - Operation Compass sweeps to El Agheila
Apr 1941 - Rommel strikes back to Sollum
Nov 1942 - Second Alamein: the Axis retreat
